title: zinnia zamudiana
intro: Zinnia zamudiana, also known as the desert marigold, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is known for its bright yellow or orange flowers. The plant has medicinal properties, with its roots and leaves used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Zinnia zamudiana include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage, and herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and salves made from the plant's roots and leaves.
